_comment: Issue #64 golden scoring dataset. Consumed by tests/relevance-scorer-evals.spec.ts. Profile interests are fixed in the spec. ageHours -> postedAt = NOW - ageHours*3600e3. expectMin/expectMax are 0..100 bounds asserted on scoreRelevance().score. Labels document the intent.

posts.text: Databases are becoming the context engines for AI agents. Incremental view maintenance keeps agent context fresh without recomputing everything. No one does this better than Feldera — see the demo: https://feldera.com/demo

After a month wiring RAG into our agents pipeline, the real latency cost was not vector search — it was databases doing redundant context recompute on every turn. Incremental compute over local llms changed the numbers.
How are teams handling evals for rag agents over changing databases? Our context engines drift and latency creeps. Curious what local llms folks measure.
Excited to announce our new rag platform for ai agents and databases! Incremental context engines, built-in evals. Sign up now: https://example.com/signup
Reflection on incremental compute for rag agents: keeping context engines exact under deltas beats recomputing the whole view. Latency and databases evals matter.
Spent the weekend rebuilding the raised garden beds and planting tomatoes. Compost finally paying off after three seasons of patience.
Here are 5 key takeaways about rag agents and databases that will transform your context engines. Leverage these cutting-edge synergies to unlock potential.
My take on incremental compute for rag agents and context engines over databases — sharing what worked for our evals and latency.
Benchmarked rag agents across three databases for context engines: incremental compute cut latency 4x. Full evals over local llms attached.
Quick note on rag agents and databases: context engines need incremental compute or your evals lie about latency over local llms.
Pairing rag agents with incremental databases for context engines made our evals trustworthy. Latency over local llms dropped noticeably.
Notes on rag agents over databases: context engines plus incremental compute keep latency and evals honest for local llms.
Our rag agents and databases context engines tooling is live. Check it out here: https://example.com/demo — incremental compute and evals included.
Excited to announce our new gardening subscription box! Fresh seeds monthly. Sign up now: https://example.com/garden
Deep dive: rag agents treat databases as context engines; incremental compute is the unlock. We measured latency, evals, and local llms tradeoffs across a real workload.
Another solid thread on rag agents, databases, context engines, incremental compute, evals and latency for local llms.
